# AGENTS.md

> For human contributors, see [README.md](README.md).

## Project Overview

Refinex-DBFlow is planned as an internal MySQL MCP database operation gateway for AI tools such as Codex, Claude, OpenCode, and Copilot. The repository currently contains documentation and Harness planning artifacts only; application source and build files have not been scaffolded yet.

## Quick Reference

| Task | Command |
| --- | --- |
| Install dependencies | Not available yet; no Maven project exists. |
| Build | Not available yet; no `pom.xml` exists. |
| Run tests | Not available yet; no test suite exists. |
| Lint / format check | Not available yet; no formatter is configured. |
| Start dev server | Not available yet; no Spring Boot app exists. |
| Validate Harness | `python3 scripts/check_harness.py` |

## Architecture

See [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) for the full architecture map.

The approved target is a Spring Boot WebMVC MCP gateway using Spring AI MCP Streamable HTTP, Bearer Token authentication, server-side SQL policy enforcement, and persistent audit logs. Until application code exists, do not claim implementation completion from documentation alone.

## Key Patterns

- Preserve existing unmanaged docs: `README.md` and `LICENSE` are not overwritten by Harness lifecycle work.
- Design decisions live in `docs/exec-plans/specs/`; implementation plans live in `docs/exec-plans/active/`.
- MCP/database work must follow the approved wide-entry, strong-server-side-policy architecture.
- High-risk SQL policy is service-side: `DROP DATABASE` and `DROP TABLE` require YAML whitelist; `TRUNCATE` requires confirmation.
- Use `harness-verify` before claiming repository work is complete.
